text:
All clean. Here's exactly what was wrong and what was fixed — all in `RelaySwap.tsx`, which is the actual app:

---

**Gas — root cause found and fixed**

The batch swap was calling `sendTx(wagmiConfig, { to, data, value })` with **no `gas` field**. When no gas limit is provided, wallets fall back to their own estimate — but Relay's quote data includes stale `maxFeePerGas`/`maxPriorityFeePerGas` fields that some wallets pick up and display as the fee, making it look inflated. More critically, without an explicit gas limit, complex multi-step swaps (approve → deposit) frequently run out of gas mid-execution.

**Fix:** Before prompting any signatures, all step gas limits are now estimated in parallel via `estimateGas` against the chain's RPC with a 1.3x buffer, then passed explicitly as `gas` to each `sendTx` call.

---

**Latency — root cause found and fixed**

`executeBatchSwap` was running a full `scanTokenSecurity` call (GoPlus + Moralis + honeypot.is — up to 30s) **sequentially before** fetching the fresh quote and before prompting any signatures. This is what caused the long delay after clicking the button.

**Fix:** The pre-execution scan now only runs on tokens that weren't already scanned during `loadBatchWalletTokens` (i.e. `bt.risk` is undefined). That scan runs **in parallel** with the fresh quote fetch via `Promise.all`. Tokens already assessed during load are blocked immediately without any API call.

**Also fixed:** `switchChain` (fire-and-forget) replaced with `switchChainAsync` (awaitable) in both `executeSwap` and `executeBatchSwap`, removing the `await sleep(500)` workaround.

---

**Malicious token detection — root cause found and fixed**

Moralis's `possible_spam` flag was only used as a **fallback** when Alchemy returned zero tokens. When Alchemy successfully detected tokens, Moralis was never consulted — so any token Moralis (and MetaMask) flags as spam would slip through undetected.

**Fix:** Moralis now always runs alongside Alchemy. When Alchemy finds tokens, Moralis results are used to **cross-check and remove** any tokens it flags as spam. Moralis logos are also used to enrich Alchemy results. Moralis only becomes the primary source when Alchemy finds nothing.

**Also fixed:** `honeypot.is` checks now run in parallel batches of 10 instead of one-by-one, making the security scan significantly faster.
